description_long: This object is a classic portable boombox radio receiver, featuring a rectangular body with a metallic and plastic exterior. The front face displays two large circular speaker grilles on either side of a central cassette tape deck, flanked by a row of tactile buttons and sliders above for tuning and control. Analog VU meters and a frequency indicator are visible above the cassette slot, providing real-time feedback for tuning and audio levels. The top panel is equipped with several rotary knobs and push buttons, likely for volume, tuning, and function selection. The sides of the device have ribbed vent-like structures and rotary dials, while the back is mostly flat with additional venting and a battery compartment. The overall design is reminiscent of 1980s urban street culture, making it a recognizable and iconic object in public spaces.
description: A retro portable boombox radio receiver with dual speakers, a central cassette deck, and multiple control knobs and buttons. It features a metallic and plastic body with prominent front-facing audio and tuning elements.
description_view_0: Front view showing dual speaker grilles, central cassette deck, analog meters, and a row of control buttons and sliders.
description_view_1: Left side view highlighting a rotary dial and ribbed vent structure, with the top control panel partially visible.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying a flat surface with venting panels and a battery compartment, lacking interactive controls.
description_view_3: Right side view featuring another rotary dial and vent structure, mirroring the left side with slight variations in button placement.
